methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Ranovus is a Series D+ frontier-tech company in Chips & Semiconductors, founded by Hamid Arabzadeh, that has raised $236M including a $36M strategic investment fund grant in August 2025. The valuation universe comprises 3 public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers, though the narrower peer set—same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier—yields insufficient datapoints for reliable median anchoring.

Two valuation triangulation methods were assessed. Peer-Median analysis is not applicable: while 14 same-sector private peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, applying strict stage and tier filters produces fewer than the required 3 comparable datapoints, making a sector-wide median unreliable for a frontier-tech entrant. Public-Comp Anchoring using the 3 semiconductor tickers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) is also not applicable because Ranovus does not disclose revenue, precluding a price-to-sales calculation. The public sector median P/S multiple stands at 25.3x across the three tickers, but without target revenue, this anchor cannot be deployed.

The sparse comparable universe and absence of disclosed financials substantially constrain quantitative valuation precision. Without applicable methods yielding a triangulated range, further analysis requires either disclosure of Ranovus revenue or identification of additional same-stage private datapoints. Key uncertainties remain the company's revenue scale, gross margins, and time-to-positive unit economics relative to the identified peer set (Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova, Credo).
